arena_scorer_hardening.target_quantity: live scorer parity must distinguish exact local-versus-official numerical comparison from liveness-only checks
forbidden_claim_patterns: live verifiers match local implementations to machine precision

fm_stretched_lr.target_quantity: signs of coefficients of the stretching polynomial t -> c^(t lambda)_(t mu,t nu), not nonnegativity of the evaluated LR values

erdos_21_q6.target_quantity: the minimum edge count q(6), with witness validity requiring pairwise intersection and cover number exactly 6; a single valid hypergraph is only an upper bound, while a lower-bound increase requires complete replayable nonexistence coverage and cannot follow from a partial or merely well-sharded no-hit search
forbidden_claim_patterns: establishes? the exact value of q\(6\)

erdos_1029_r55.target_quantity: the finite diagonal Ramsey bracket 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46; a graph witness and a nonexistence certificate move opposite sides of the bracket; for an undirected C_42(S) indexed by shortest distances 1..21, degree = 2|S| - 1 when 21 is in S, so |S|=19..21 is near-complete rather than half-density

erdos_552_c4_star_n17.target_quantity: the n=17,m=22 C4-versus-star cell: either a C4-free graph of minimum degree at least 5 or a replayable nonexistence certificate. A 22-vertex witness implies R(C4,S17) >= 23, not >= 22. The displayed 21-vertex Hermitian-polarity fixture has degree range 4..5 and 48 edges; it is neither SRG(21,5,0,1) nor a minimum-degree-5 equality witness. A bounded no-hit search certifies only the tried generator outputs, not route inaccessibility or extension impossibility

erdos_140_r3_212.target_quantity: the finite value r_3(212): a 44-element 3-AP-free witness improves the lower bound; exactness needs complete nonexistence evidence. A bounded no-hit search certifies only the named implementation and seeded trials, not a numerical ceiling, route exclusion, construction necessity, or nonexistence. Repeated trials or equivalent state transitions are not independent strategies. An annealing claim requires seeded randomness and possible non-improving transitions; a cardinality-growth claim requires a move that can increase cardinality

erdos_1_a11.target_quantity: the finite distinct-subset-sum record a(11), currently 310 <= a(11) <= 594; a witness only improves the upper bound

erdos_41_b3_a11.target_quantity: the shortest 11-mark B3 ruler with all multiset triple sums distinct, currently at most 445; a witness only improves the upper bound

erdos_67_discrepancy_c3.target_quantity: the unrestricted finite C=3 discrepancy witness length beyond 130000, distinct from the exact multiplicative subcase

erdos_86_q7_c4.target_quantity: a C4-free subgraph of Q7 with at least 305 edges, which improves only the finite lower bound ex(Q7,C4) >= 305
